free_text: Gamma Rays from (n,n'gamma) Interaction of 2.8 MeV
Neutrons with Magnesium, Aluminum, Silicon, Titanium
gives a total cross section which is correct only if
the gamma-ray angular distribution is isotropic.
However, since these distributions are not isotropic,
the total cross sections are divided by 4pi and the
results are presented in differential form in units
of millibarns per steradian at 90 degree. Finally,
Formula 1 cross section: sigma=P/(phi epsilon N Sa),
where P is the number of counts in the total
absorption peak, phi the effective neutron flux,
epsilon a composite efficiency, N the number of atoms
in the sample, Sa is the self-absorption factor for
Level energies are based on transition column Table 1,
